current_focus: Treat K=128 only as the initial search anchor, not as a success criterion. The research objective is to find configurations whose FVU is dramatically better than the current K=128 baseline, with a target on the order of halving that baseline FVU before rewarding smaller K or lower cost.

recent_results.description: Advance the `jumprelu` family from prototype to stabilization with a single family-specific parameter sweep point at the K=128 anchor. Keep architecture and optimizer fixed and test a sharper thresholded encoder to see whether the current 0.1888 full FVU can be pushed materially lower before considering any cost tradeoffs.

Stay on the best known working JumpReLU K=128 configuration and run a single softer-gating proxy check by increasing gate bandwidth slightly, keeping the round focused on stability recovery and a clean read on threshold dynamics.

Stay on the best known working JumpReLU K=128 configuration and run a single softer-gating proxy check by increasing gate bandwidth slightly, keeping the round focused on stability recovery and a clean read on threshold dynamics. full

recent_round_summaries.hypothesis: A gated SAE that decouples latent support selection from reconstruction magnitude can beat the current JumpReLU anchor at K=128 by learning a separate sigmoid routing branch while staying initialization-close to plain ReLU top-k.

A softer JumpReLU gate may recover stable throughput and improve proxy FVU versus the failed sharp-gating attempt by widening the bandwidth around the current best K=128 anchor without changing architecture or optimizer.

gated.design_hypothesis: A gated SAE that decouples latent support selection from reconstruction magnitude can beat the current JumpReLU anchor at K=128 by learning a separate sigmoid routing branch while staying initialization-close to plain ReLU top-k.
next_steps: If gated shows better proxy FVU at acceptable throughput, stabilize the family by exposing and tuning gate temperature/init strength in a follow-up.
If gated quality is promising but throughput regresses, keep the family alive and do a performance-focused follow-up rather than discarding the architecture.
If gated fails to improve proxy FVU, move on to a different routing-focused family instead of more JumpReLU-local sweeps.

recent_performance_findings: round 3: suspected performance regression for jumprelu (tps=16601.650675, baseline_ratio=0.200000, termination=throughput_too_low)
next_move_guidance: If wider-bandwidth JumpReLU improves proxy FVU with normal throughput, continue stabilizing within JumpReLU with another small gate-shape adjustment before any K reduction.
If throughput recovers but quality is flat or worse, try a small LR adjustment on the best JumpReLU anchor rather than more aggressive threshold changes.
If this softer-gating proxy still underperforms, pause JumpReLU-local sweeps until crash recovery ends and then revisit broader architecture exploration.
